last_item: web/Extensions — the Tier-3 ('needs_restart') extension-install gate in web/src/views/Extensions.tsx: 'Install for me' triggers a server-wide `systemctl restart postgresql` (every database briefly down) and is gated behind a typed-name confirm (confirmOk = !needsRestart || typed === ext.name, Extensions.tsx:452; disabled at :519). There was NO Extensions.test.tsx, so the confirm-on-restart invariant was entirely unproven (band-1 correctness)
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): added web/src/views/Extensions.test.tsx (2 tests) driving the real <Extensions /> (mocks only sonner + the api.* loads). Test 1 opens the Tier-3 Add dialog and pins the gate: DISABLED on open, and for a DIFFERENT name (postgis), a prefix/substring (pg), a SUPERSTRING (pg_cron2 — kills .includes/.startsWith loosening), and a whitespace-padded value ( pg_cron  — locks exact === with no .trim() here, unlike Version); only exact pg_cron ENABLES it, and installExtension fires only after with confirm:'pg_cron'; asserts the server-wide-restart consequence copy is on screen first. Test 2 pins the safe path: a Tier-1 (ready) add opens NO dialog and posts confirm:'' — never a restart token. No bug — both paths correct; tests lock the contract. Mutation-proven over 7 one-line mutations (perl apply + git checkout revert, tree clean each time): loosen === to .startsWith(name)/name.startsWith(typed)/!=='' ; drop !confirmOk from disabled; swap confirm token to ''  :typed; invert ext.tier==='ready' routing; needsRestart=false. code-reviewer clean (faithful real-path test, correct API-arg expectations, matches Version/RolesDatabases conventions). test-skeptic flagged confirm:typed->confirm:ext.name as a passing mutation; I VERIFIED and REJECTED it as behavior-preserving — the button only enables when typed===ext.name so the two emit the identical wire value on every reachable path, AND the server independently re-checks (core.RequireConfirmation -> exact Typed==name, extensions.go:292); it's a client-side refactor, not a safety break, and no UI-driven test can distinguish them without pinning implementation detail. Recorded as an active rule in learnings.md. Web gate green: typecheck clean, build OK, 169/169 vitest (+2). Backend untouched (no Go changed). e2e N/A (pure RTL).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
